business.headline: Sensex ends 1,097 points lower, Nifty below 24,500; SBI down 3%Sensex ends 1,097 points lower, Nifty below 24,500; SBI down 3%

Mukesh Ambani leads India's richest, Gautam Adani second: Hurun Global Rich List 2026Mukesh Ambani continues to hold the title of India's richest person and also remains Asia's wealthiest individual. His total wealth rose by about 9% over the past year to reach Rs 9.8 lakh crore.

For many retail investors, this has meant seeing their mutual fund or stock market portfolios slip into the red.

Some operate small enterprises, others are building growing ventures, but each one contributes to livelihoods, job creation, and the strength of local economies.

Sensex down 1,100 points: Why is stock market falling today?The S&P BSE Sensex was down 566.31 points at 79,449.59, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 164.85 points to 24,601.05 as of 11:24 am. Sensex and Nifty shed over half a percent.

Gulf crisis disrupts LNG supply and your kitchen may feel the heatIf LNG supply disruptions persist, the impact may soon reach your kitchen, as higher global gas prices gradually filter into household gas bills and CNG costs across Indian cities.

These include investment pledges to foreign governments or companies, sports sponsorship agreements, contracts with businesses and investors, and even the possible sale of certain holdings.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market rise or fall today?Gift Nifty futures were trading at 24,649 at around 8:10 am, indicating that the benchmark Nifty 50 may open below Thursday's closing level of 24,765.9.

Rs 10,000 in PPF or SIP? Which builds more wealth in 10 yearsA simple annual investment of Rs 10,000 may not sound like much, but over time it can grow into a meaningful sum. The real question is where to invest it, i.e., the predictable returns of PPF or the market-linked potential of a SIP. So which one comes out ahead after ten years? Let's have a look!

No LPG or LNG shortage, India expands talks with global energy suppliersIndia currently imports about 195 million metric standard cubic metres per day (MMSCMD) of natural gas. Out of this, around 60 MMSCMD comes from Qatar. However, authorities said India is already exploring alternative markets to reduce dependence on any single supplier.

Sensex surges 900 points, Nifty tops 24,750; Reliance jumps 3%The Sensex ended the session 900 points higher, while the Nifty closed above 24,750, as investors stepped in to buy beaten-down stocks following the recent market correction.

Will Iran tensions shake Dubai's booming real estate market?Rising tensions involving Iran and parts of the Gulf are now testing that perception, with reports of attacks reaching areas of the UAE and raising questions about whether the region's instability could shake one of the world's hottest property markets.

Why do only 20% of women reach senior roles in the same company vs 49% men?Many organisations hire women in good numbers, yet far fewer make it to leadership positions within the same company. The gap raises an important question: what happens along the way that slows down women's career progression?

Adani Total Gas hikes industrial gas prices amid Middle East tensionsThe supply challenges come after attacks on Iran and retaliatory strikes by Tehran, which have disrupted shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. The narrow waterway between Iran and Oman is one of the world's most important energy routes.

Still think rural women aren't financially savvy? See how they manage moneyThe common belief is that rural women are not financially aware. Yet daily life in many households tells a different story. Women are budgeting carefully, building small savings and guiding family spending. Could rural women actually be the silent managers of household finances?

Mona Singh says brotherhood culture everywhere makes women feel like outsiders Mona Singh shed light on real-life gender bias reflected in Kohrra 2. In a recent interview, the actor addressed the "brotherhood romance" in workplaces and other traditional attitudes that affect women.

Indian origin Geeta Gandbhir on Oscar-nominated documentary, The Perfect Neighbor Indian-American filmmaker Geeta Gandbhir spoke to India Today about her Oscar-nominated documentary The Perfect Neighbor, which reconstructs the tragic killing of Ajika Owens in Florida using police body-camera footage.

Janhvi Kapoor walks 3,500 steps to Tirumala shrine on her birthday. Watch Janhvi Kapoor marked her 29th birthday with a traditional pilgrimage to Tirumala. She reportedly fulfilled a personal vow and continued her spiritual practices with family and friends.

Govinda reacts to criticism over small shows and school event performances Bollywood actor Govinda has responded to social media criticism regarding his appearances at school events, emphasizing that his choices are not influenced by ego and highlighting the significance of the occasions he attended.

general news.headline: Why Trump is wary of creating another China in IndiaHandcrafted ByOur Editors The US seems to have suddenly woken up to the 'mistakes' it made while dealing with China 20 years ago. It resulted in China being the economic powerhouse it is today. Now, Trump wants to avoid repeating those with India. But India never needed anyone for its rise.

Live: Balen Shah marches ahead with leads on 2/3rd seats, rivals far behindNepal Election Results 2026 Live Updates: Vote counting is underway in Nepal's high-stakes general election in which rapper-turned-politician Balendra Shah's party gained leads in early counting. Out of a total of 275 seats in Nepal's lower house of parliament, counting is in progress for 165 seats that are elected by direct election. The remaining 110 seats will be filled later via a system of proportional representation. Voting took place on Thursday, months after a Gen Z-led protest movement forced Oli and his Communist Party of Nepal (Unified Marxist–Leninist) government out of power in September 2025. Stay tuned to indiatoday.in for the latest updates.

Meet Anuj Agnihotri, doctor from Rajasthan who topped UPSC Civil Services 2026MBBS graduate Anuj Agnihotri has secured All India Rank 1 in the UPSC Civil Services Examination 2026. Originally from a small town in Rajasthan, his journey from medicine to public administration reflects years of preparation, policy thinking and a strong interest in field-based governance and public service.

Live: Azerbaijan withdraws its diplomats from Iran day after drone strikesUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: On Day 7, the war in the Middle East intensified as Iran launched fresh missile attacks on Israel and US bases, with Israel intercepting incoming strikes. Israeli forces also hit Hezbollah positions in Lebanon, where at least eight people were killed. The conflict has killed more than 1,230 people in Iran, over 70 in Lebanon and about a dozen in Israel, while six U.S. troops have died. The U.S. Embassy in Kuwait shut down after Iranian retaliatory strikes. The fighting has rattled global markets, pushed oil prices higher and slowed shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, while U.S. President Donald Trump signalled interest in influencing Iran's next leadership. Stay tuned to IndiaToday.in for live updates on the ongoing Middle East conflict.

He made Pak nuclear power, but Pak bombed his memoryPakistan's only Nobel laureate, Abdus Salam, helped build the foundations of the country's nuclear programme. Because he belonged to the Ahmadiyya sect, his religious identity was stripped away, and his legacy was erased in Pakistan. Islamabad boasts of its nuclear weapons, but gives no credit to Salam.

Dubai residents get possible missile attack alert on phones, urged to avoid outdoorsSeveral Middle East countries, including the UAE, have come under attack by Iran, which has been hitting US targets in retaliation to the US-Israeli aggression against it over the past week.

How the India-Canada nuclear pact exorcises the ghost of TrudeausIndia and Canada are moving to reset ties through a landmark civil nuclear agreement, decades after relations were ruptured by India's use of a Canadian reactor for the 1974 Pokhran nuclear test. The pact also closes a turbulent chapter shaped by Canadian PMs Pierre Trudeau and his son Justin.

Vijay, Trisha attend wedding together days after wife alleges affair with actorVijay and Trisha Krishnan attended the wedding reception of Kalpathi Suresh and Meenakshi Suresh's son. This is their first appearance amid the Jana Nayagan actor's divorce case.

The 'missile from space' that killed KhameneiIsrael reportedly used the Blue Sparrow, a missile that briefly exits the atmosphere before falling almost vertically onto its target, to kill Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in Tehran on February 28, 2026. US releases missing Epstein files with minor's assault claim against TrumpThe disclosure comes as the Justice Department faces congressional scrutiny over its handling of documents from the Epstein investigation that must be made public. Nishant Kumar to be made JDU chief, Nitish Kumar to hold key party meet todayAccording to sources, many within the party believe Nishant Kumar's entry into active politics could help maintain unity and prevent potential splits at a time when the JD(U) faces a delicate political phase.

Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.
